name: Six Sigma DMAIC Ontology (DMAIC-ONT)
description: Ontology for modelling Six Sigma DMAIC — the standard 8-24 week project-based process improvement methodology with full statistical rigour. Models the Define-Measure-Analyze-Improve-Control lifecycle including SIPOC scoping, CTQ (Critical to Quality) trees, Gage R&R measurement system analysis, process capability (Cp/Cpk/sigma/DPMO), root cause analysis with hypothesis testing, design of experiments (DOE), and SPC control plans. Imports PE-ONT v4.0.0 for Process, ProcessMetric, AIAgent, Skill, Hypothesis, and ProcessPattern reuse.
oaa:domain: Six Sigma, DMAIC, Statistical Process Control, Process Capability, Design of Experiments

creator.name: Platform Foundation Core - Ontology Architect Agent

oaa:joinPatterns.name: VOC-to-Sigma Pipeline
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Trace the full Define-Measure chain from customer voice through CTQ specification to validated measurement system and baseline process capability
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: dmaic:SixSigmaProject → hasSIPOC → dmaic:SIPOC ← ctqFromSIPOC ← dmaic:CriticalToQuality → validatedByMSA → dmaic:MeasurementSystem → msaForCapability → dmaic:ProcessCapability
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Answer: What is the baseline sigma level for the CTQs identified from the customer voice?

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Root-Cause-to-Proof Chain
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Trace from validated root cause through experimental confirmation to demonstrated capability improvement
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Answer: Which experiments confirmed the root causes and what capability improvement was achieved?

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Cross-Ontology Process Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Connect the Six Sigma project to the PE-ONT process it improves and the metrics that track improvement
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: dmaic:SixSigmaProject → appliesToProcess → pe:Process → pe:measures → pe:ProcessMetric ← improvesMeasure ← dmaic:SixSigmaProject
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Answer: Which PE process metrics improved as a result of this Six Sigma project?

entities.name: SixSigmaProject
entities.description: A Six Sigma DMAIC improvement project spanning 8-24 weeks. Contains the project charter (problem statement, business case, scope, team), sigma targets, and tracks progression through Define-Measure-Analyze-Improve-Control phases. The central coordinating entity that owns all other DMAIC artefacts.

oaa:properties.name: problemStatement
oaa:properties.description: Clear statement of the problem being addressed — what, where, when, magnitude

oaa:properties.name: businessCase
oaa:properties.description: Financial or strategic justification for the project

oaa:properties.name: scope
oaa:properties.description: In-scope and out-of-scope boundaries

oaa:properties.name: beltLevel
oaa:properties.description: Six Sigma belt level of the project lead

oaa:properties.name: teamLead
oaa:properties.description: Project lead (belt holder)

oaa:properties.name: champion
oaa:properties.description: Executive sponsor / champion authorising the project

oaa:properties.name: teamSize
oaa:properties.description: Number of cross-functional team members

oaa:properties.name: baselineSigma
oaa:properties.description: Sigma level at project start (populated after Measure phase)

oaa:properties.name: targetSigma
oaa:properties.description: Target sigma level the project aims to achieve

oaa:properties.name: currentSigma
oaa:properties.description: Current sigma level (updated through project lifecycle)

oaa:properties.name: targetEndDate
oaa:properties.description: Planned completion date (advisory 8-24 weeks per BR-D-007)

oaa:properties.name: phase
oaa:properties.description: Current DMAIC phase

oaa:properties.name: estimatedSaving
oaa:properties.description: Estimated annual cost saving or revenue impact

entities.name: SIPOC
entities.description: Supplier-Input-Process-Output-Customer diagram created during the Define phase. Provides a high-level view of the process boundaries and key stakeholders. Must be defined before any CTQ identification (BR-D-001).

oaa:properties.name: suppliers
oaa:properties.description: Entities providing inputs to the process (comma-separated or structured list)

oaa:properties.name: inputs
oaa:properties.description: Materials, information, or resources entering the process

oaa:properties.name: processSteps
oaa:properties.description: High-level process steps (5-7 major steps)

oaa:properties.name: outputs
oaa:properties.description: Products, services, or deliverables produced

oaa:properties.name: customers
oaa:properties.description: Recipients of the process outputs

entities.name: CriticalToQuality
entities.description: A Critical to Quality (CTQ) characteristic derived from the Voice of the Customer (VOC). Each CTQ has measurable specification limits (USL, LSL, target) that define acceptable quality. The CTQ tree traces from customer need → driver → measurable CTQ. Used to focus measurement and improvement efforts on what matters most to the customer.

oaa:properties.name: vocSource
oaa:properties.description: Voice of the Customer source (surveys, complaints, interviews, market data)

oaa:properties.name: driver
oaa:properties.description: Customer need or quality driver this CTQ addresses

oaa:properties.name: unit
oaa:properties.description: Unit of measurement (e.g. mm, seconds, %, ppm)

oaa:properties.name: upperSpecLimit
oaa:properties.description: Upper Specification Limit (USL)

oaa:properties.name: lowerSpecLimit
oaa:properties.description: Lower Specification Limit (LSL)

oaa:properties.name: target
oaa:properties.description: Target value (nominal specification)

oaa:properties.name: priority
oaa:properties.description: Priority based on impact to customer satisfaction

entities.name: MeasurementSystem
entities.description: Measurement System Analysis (MSA) — a Gage R&R study validating that the measurement system is adequate for the CTQ being measured. %GRR must be ≤ 30% for the system to be acceptable (BR-D-002). Without a validated measurement system, capability data is unreliable.

oaa:properties.name: studyType
oaa:properties.description: Type of MSA study conducted

oaa:properties.name: operators
oaa:properties.description: Number of operators/appraisers in the study

oaa:properties.name: parts
oaa:properties.description: Number of parts/samples measured

oaa:properties.name: trials
oaa:properties.description: Number of measurement trials per operator-part combination

oaa:properties.name: repeatability
oaa:properties.description: Equipment variation (EV) — variation from the gauge itself

oaa:properties.name: reproducibility
oaa:properties.description: Appraiser variation (AV) — variation between operators

oaa:properties.name: percentGRR
oaa:properties.description: Total Gage R&R as percentage of tolerance. Must be ≤ 30% (BR-D-002). ≤ 10% ideal, 10-30% acceptable, > 30% unacceptable.

oaa:properties.name: numberOfDistinctCategories
oaa:properties.description: NDC — must be ≥ 5 for adequate discrimination

oaa:properties.name: acceptable
oaa:properties.description: Whether the measurement system passes the %GRR ≤ 30% threshold

entities.name: ProcessCapability
entities.description: Process capability assessment capturing sigma level, Cp, Cpk (short-term capability), Pp, Ppk (long-term performance), DPMO, and yield. Baseline capability is established during Measure phase; current capability is tracked through Improve and Control phases to demonstrate sustained improvement. Current sigma must exceed baseline for project closure (BR-D-008).

oaa:properties.name: capabilityType
oaa:properties.description: Whether this is the baseline measurement, current state, or target

oaa:properties.name: sigmaLevel
oaa:properties.description: Process sigma level (e.g. 3.2, 4.5, 6.0)

oaa:properties.name: cp
oaa:properties.description: Process Capability index (Cp) — potential capability assuming centred process

oaa:properties.name: cpk
oaa:properties.description: Process Capability index (Cpk) — actual capability accounting for centering. Cpk ≥ 1.33 is generally capable.

oaa:properties.name: pp
oaa:properties.description: Process Performance index (Pp) — long-term potential

oaa:properties.name: ppk
oaa:properties.description: Process Performance index (Ppk) — long-term actual

oaa:properties.name: dpmo
oaa:properties.description: Defects Per Million Opportunities

oaa:properties.name: yield
oaa:properties.description: First-pass yield as percentage (0-100)

oaa:properties.name: sampleSize
oaa:properties.description: Number of data points in the capability study

oaa:properties.name: collectionDate
oaa:properties.description: When the capability data was collected

entities.name: RootCauseAnalysis
entities.description: Root cause identification and validation performed during the Analyze phase. Combines qualitative methods (fishbone/Ishikawa, 5-Why) with statistical hypothesis testing to confirm root causes. Only statistically validated root causes (p < 0.05) proceed to the Improve phase (BR-D-004). Requires baseline capability to exist first (BR-D-003).

oaa:properties.name: method
oaa:properties.description: Root cause analysis method used

oaa:properties.name: suspectedCause
oaa:properties.description: The suspected root cause being investigated

oaa:properties.name: category
oaa:properties.description: Ishikawa 6M category of the root cause

oaa:properties.name: statisticalTest
oaa:properties.description: Statistical test used (e.g. t-test, chi-square, ANOVA, regression)

oaa:properties.name: pValue
oaa:properties.description: p-value from hypothesis test. Must be < 0.05 for validation (BR-D-004).

oaa:properties.name: validated
oaa:properties.description: Whether the root cause has been statistically validated

oaa:properties.name: rpn
oaa:properties.description: Risk Priority Number from FMEA (Severity × Occurrence × Detection, 1-1000)

entities.name: Experiment
entities.description: A Design of Experiments (DOE) study conducted during the Improve phase. Tests multiple factors simultaneously to identify optimal settings and interactions. Unlike Kaizen rapid experiments (hours), DOE runs over days/weeks with rigorous statistical design. At least one experiment must be completed before creating control plans (BR-D-005).

oaa:properties.name: designType
oaa:properties.description: Type of experimental design

oaa:properties.name: factors
oaa:properties.description: Factors (variables) being tested (comma-separated list)

oaa:properties.name: levels
oaa:properties.description: Number of levels per factor (typically 2 or 3)

oaa:properties.name: runs
oaa:properties.description: Total number of experimental runs

oaa:properties.name: replicates
oaa:properties.description: Number of replicates per run

oaa:properties.name: significantFactors
oaa:properties.description: Factors found to be statistically significant (p < 0.05)

oaa:properties.name: interactions
oaa:properties.description: Significant factor interactions identified

oaa:properties.name: optimalSettings
oaa:properties.description: Optimal factor settings determined from the experiment

oaa:properties.name: result
oaa:properties.description: Overall experiment outcome

oaa:properties.name: rSquared
oaa:properties.description: R-squared value from the experimental model (0-1)

entities.name: ControlPlan
entities.description: A Statistical Process Control (SPC) plan created during the Control phase to sustain improvements. Specifies the SPC chart type, control limits (UCL/LCL), monitoring frequency, reaction plan for out-of-control conditions, and responsible owner. Every control plan must monitor at least one CTQ (BR-D-006).

oaa:properties.name: spcChartType
oaa:properties.description: Type of SPC chart used for monitoring

oaa:properties.name: ucl
oaa:properties.description: Upper Control Limit

oaa:properties.name: lcl
oaa:properties.description: Lower Control Limit

oaa:properties.name: centerLine
oaa:properties.description: Center line (process mean or median)

oaa:properties.name: monitorFrequency
oaa:properties.description: How often samples are taken (e.g. 'hourly', 'per-shift', 'daily', 'weekly')

oaa:properties.name: sampleSize
oaa:properties.description: Number of observations per sample

oaa:properties.name: reactionPlan
oaa:properties.description: Actions to take when an out-of-control condition is detected

oaa:properties.name: responsibleOwner
oaa:properties.description: Person or role responsible for monitoring and reacting

oaa:properties.name: effectiveDate
oaa:properties.description: Date when control plan monitoring begins

relationships.name: Has SIPOC
relationships.description: A Six Sigma project defines its process boundaries via a SIPOC diagram. Must be completed before CTQ identification (BR-D-001).

relationships.name: Defines CTQ
relationships.description: A Six Sigma project identifies one or more Critical to Quality characteristics from the Voice of the Customer

relationships.name: Validated By MSA
relationships.description: A CTQ's measurement system is validated via a Gage R&R study before data collection begins

relationships.name: Baseline Capability
relationships.description: The baseline process capability established during the Measure phase. Required before root cause analysis (BR-D-003).

relationships.name: Current Capability
relationships.description: The current process capability tracked through Improve and Control phases

relationships.name: Has Root Cause
relationships.description: A Six Sigma project identifies and validates root causes during the Analyze phase

relationships.name: Tested By Experiment
relationships.description: A root cause's countermeasure is tested through a formal Design of Experiments study

relationships.name: Confirms Improvement
relationships.description: An experiment confirms capability improvement by producing a new ProcessCapability assessment

relationships.name: Sustained By Control
relationships.description: A Six Sigma project sustains improvements through one or more SPC control plans

relationships.name: Control Monitors
relationships.description: A control plan monitors one or more CTQ characteristics via SPC charts. Every control plan must monitor at least one CTQ (BR-D-006).

relationships.name: MSA For Capability
relationships.description: A measurement system analysis validates the measurement system used for a specific capability study

relationships.name: CTQ From SIPOC
relationships.description: A CTQ traces back to the SIPOC that defined the process outputs and customer requirements

relationships.name: Applies To Process
relationships.description: A Six Sigma project applies to (improves) a specific PE-ONT process. Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Improves Measure
relationships.description: A Six Sigma project improves one or more PE-ONT process metrics. Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Augmented By Agent
relationships.description: A Six Sigma project is augmented by AI agents for data analysis, capability calculation, or DOE optimisation. Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Invokes Skill
relationships.description: An experiment invokes analytical skills during execution (e.g. DOE analysis, SPC calculation). Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Tests Hypothesis
relationships.description: A root cause analysis tests a formal PE-ONT hypothesis with statistical evidence. Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Follows Pattern
relationships.description: A Six Sigma project follows reusable process patterns (e.g. DMAIC cycle, tollgate reviews). Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

businessRules.name: SIPOC Before CTQ
businessRules.description: A SIPOC diagram must be defined before any CTQ characteristics are identified — you cannot define quality requirements without understanding the process boundaries.
businessRules.condition: IF a CriticalToQuality entity is created for a SixSigmaProject
businessRules.action: THEN the SixSigmaProject MUST already have a hasSIPOC relationship to a SIPOC entity

businessRules.name: Measurement System Adequacy
businessRules.description: The measurement system must have %GRR ≤ 30% to be acceptable. Data collected with an inadequate measurement system cannot be trusted for capability analysis.
businessRules.condition: IF a MeasurementSystem is used for capability assessment
businessRules.action: THEN MeasurementSystem.percentGRR MUST be ≤ 30 AND MeasurementSystem.acceptable MUST be true

businessRules.name: Baseline Before Root Cause Analysis
businessRules.description: Baseline process capability must be established before root cause analysis begins — without a baseline, you cannot quantify the gap to close.
businessRules.condition: IF a RootCauseAnalysis entity is created for a SixSigmaProject
businessRules.action: THEN the SixSigmaProject MUST already have a baselineCapability relationship to a ProcessCapability entity with capabilityType='baseline'

businessRules.name: Statistical Validation Required
businessRules.description: Root causes must be statistically validated (p < 0.05) before proceeding to the Improve phase. Opinion-based root causes are insufficient.
businessRules.action: THEN RootCauseAnalysis.pValue MUST be < 0.05 OR method MUST be one of [fishbone, 5-why] with supporting evidence

businessRules.name: Experiment Before Control Plan
businessRules.description: At least one experiment must be completed before control plans can be created — you cannot control what you have not improved.
businessRules.condition: IF a ControlPlan entity is created for a SixSigmaProject
businessRules.action: THEN at least one Experiment MUST exist with result in ['success', 'partial']

businessRules.name: Control Plan Must Monitor CTQ
businessRules.description: Every control plan must monitor at least one CTQ characteristic — a control plan without a target metric is meaningless.
businessRules.action: THEN the ControlPlan MUST have at least one controlMonitors relationship to a CriticalToQuality entity

businessRules.name: Project Duration Advisory
businessRules.description: DMAIC projects typically span 8-24 weeks. Shorter projects may be better suited for Kaizen (K-DMAIC-ONT); longer projects may indicate scope creep.
businessRules.condition: IF a SixSigmaProject duration (targetEndDate minus startDate) is < 8 weeks OR > 24 weeks
businessRules.action: THEN system SHOULD generate advisory warning about atypical project duration

businessRules.name: Sigma Improvement Required for Closure
businessRules.description: A project cannot be closed unless current sigma exceeds baseline sigma — the whole point of DMAIC is measurable improvement.
businessRules.condition: IF a SixSigmaProject phase transitions to 'closed'
businessRules.action: THEN SixSigmaProject.currentSigma MUST be greater than SixSigmaProject.baselineSigma

competencyQuestions.question: What are the Critical to Quality characteristics and their specification limits (USL/LSL/target)?
Is the measurement system adequate for the CTQ being measured (%GRR ≤ 30%)?
What is the baseline process capability (Cp, Cpk, sigma level, DPMO)?
What root causes were statistically validated (p < 0.05) and in which Ishikawa category?
What DOE experiments were run and which factors were statistically significant?
What is the before/after sigma improvement achieved by the project?
Which SPC control plans sustain the improvements and what are their control limits?
What are the SIPOC boundaries and which PE-ONT process metrics improved?

testInstances.description: Monitors subgroup means (X-bar) and ranges (R) for variable data with small subgroups (n=2-10). Most common SPC chart for manufacturing processes.
testInstances.reactionPlan: Stop production, identify assignable cause, correct before resuming

testInstances.description: Monitors subgroup means and standard deviations for variable data with larger subgroups (n>10). More powerful than X-bar R for larger samples.
testInstances.reactionPlan: Investigate shift in mean or variability, adjust process parameters

testInstances.description: Monitors individual measurements and moving ranges for processes where subgrouping is impractical (batch processes, slow output, destructive testing).
testInstances.reactionPlan: Review batch conditions, isolate affected output, re-centre process

testInstances.description: Monitors the proportion of defective items in variable-size samples. Used for attribute data (pass/fail, go/no-go) where sample sizes may vary.
testInstances.reactionPlan: Investigate spike in defective proportion, review incoming material quality

testInstances.description: Monitors the count of defective items in constant-size samples. Simpler than p-chart when sample size is fixed.
testInstances.reactionPlan: If count exceeds UCL, quarantine lot and investigate root cause

testInstances.description: Monitors the total count of defects per constant inspection unit. Used when multiple defects can occur on a single item (e.g. scratches per panel, errors per form).
testInstances.reactionPlan: If defect count exceeds UCL, perform line inspection and tool check

testInstances.description: Monitors defects per unit when inspection area or sample size varies. Generalisation of c-chart for variable opportunity.
testInstances.reactionPlan: Review variation in defect density, adjust process or materials

testInstances.description: Monitors cumulative deviations from the target mean. More sensitive to small, sustained shifts in process mean than Shewhart charts. Used for high-precision processes.
testInstances.reactionPlan: Investigate gradual drift, adjust process to re-centre on target

changeControl.changeNotes: v1.0.0: Initial creation. 8 entity types (SixSigmaProject, SIPOC, CriticalToQuality, MeasurementSystem, ProcessCapability, RootCauseAnalysis, Experiment, ControlPlan). 18 relationships (12 internal + 6 cross-ontology to PE-ONT). 8 business rules. 8 competency questions. 3 join patterns. 8 predefined SPC chart type instances. Imports PE-ONT v4.0.0.
